blocks:
Ayah

يُسَبِّحُ لِلَّهِ مَا فِى ٱلسَّمَـٰوَٰتِ وَمَا فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ ٱلْمَلِكِ ٱلْقُدُّوسِ ٱلْعَزِيزِ ٱلْحَكِيمِ (۱)
Whatever there is in the heavens and in the earth glorifies Allah, the Sovereign, the All-holy, the All-mighty, the All-wise. (1)

Key Points

The previous Surah began with Sabbih (Glorify) and this Surah began with Yusabbihu (Glorifies), perhaps indicating that glorification (Tasbih) for Allah was, is, and will be.

Perhaps the four attributes, «الملك، القدوس، العزیز، الحكیم» (the Sovereign, the Holy, the Mighty, the Wise), in this Ayah are an introduction to the next Ayah which states: «هو الّذى بعث فى الامّیّین...» (It is He Who has sent among the unlettered...). If Allah sends a Prophet for you, it is not because of Allah's need for worship, but rather He is a pure, mighty Sovereign, and the sending of the Prophets is based on His wisdom, which is to bring humanity from darkness into light through purification (tazkiyah), self-building, and the teaching and instruction of religion and wisdom.

The glorification of Allah is the root and foundation of all correct Islamic beliefs and thoughts: Monotheism (Tawhid) is based on glorification and declaring Allah free from association (Shirk). «سبحان اللّه عمّا یشركون»(1) Justice (Adl) is based on declaring Allah free from oppression. «سبحان ربّنا انّا كنّا ظالمین»(2) Prophethood and Imamate are based on the glorification of Allah, meaning Allah is too pure to abandon humanity without a guide and leave them to their own devices. Therefore, those who say that Allah has not sent revelation or guidance have not truly recognized Him. «و ما قدروا اللّه حقّ قدره اذ قالوا ما انزل اللّه»(3) Resurrection (Ma'ad) is based on the glorification of Allah and declaring Him free from futile, vain, or false actions. «ربّنا ما خلقت هذا باطلاً سبحانك»(4), «افحسبتم انّما خلقناكم عبثاً و انّكم الینا لا ترجعون»(5) Yes, if a person declares Allah free from every fault and imperfection, he becomes His lover and servant, fears Him, relies upon Him, and organizes his social relations, movements, and actions according to His pleasure.

The importance and status of glorification (Tasbih) in the Quran: The command for reliance (Tawakkul) has appeared 8 times, the command for seeking forgiveness (Istighfar) 8 times, the command for worship 5 times, the command for remembrance (Dhikr) of God 5 times, the command for magnification (Takbir) 2 times, and the command for prostration (Sujud) several times, but the command for glorification (Tasbih) has appeared 16 times.

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام narrates from the noble Messenger of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله that he said: Whenever a servant says Subhan Allah, everything under the Throne glorifies along with him, and the speaker of this word is given ten times the reward. And whenever he says Alhamdulillah, Allah bestows upon him the blessings of this world until the time he meets Allah, and then the blessings of the Hereafter are bestowed upon him.(6)

The glorification of Allah is a type of gratitude towards Him. The Quran states: Whenever victory and conquest come to you, glorify God. «اذا جاء نصراللّه والفتح... فسبّح بحمد ربّك»(7)

The glorification of Allah is an expiation for the words spoken or heard in gatherings. We read in a Hadith: When the Messenger of God صلى الله عليه وآله would rise from gatherings, He would recite the dhikr «سبحانك الّلهم و بحمدك» and say: This word is the expiation for the gathering. «انّه كفّارة المجلس»

Glorification is a means of salvation. The Quran says about Prophet Yunus عليه السلام: «فلولا انّه كان من المسبّحین للبث فى بطنه الى یوم یبعثون»(8) If he had not been one of those who glorify, he would have remained in its belly until the Day they are resurrected.

In another Hadith, we read: When a person says Subhan Allah, all the angels send blessings upon him. «صلّى علیه كل ملك»(9)

During the pre-dawn hours (sahars) of the blessed month of Ramadan, it is recommended to recite a supplication whose every sentence begins with Subhan Allah: «سبحان من یعلم جوارح القلوب...، سبحان ربّ الودود...»

We read in a Hadith: A person entered the house of Imam Sadiq عليه السلام and saw the Imam performing prayer, repeating the dhikr Subhan Allah sixty times. Or we read: Imam Sadiq عليه السلام repeated the dhikr Subhan Allah five hundred times while in prostration.(10)

The Glorification of Creatures In the divine worldview, worship and servitude to Allah are not exclusive to humans; rather, all creatures are in a state of worship. A person requested a miracle from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله. The Prophet picked up some pebbles from the ground, held them in His hand, and, by the Prophet's request and Allah's permission, they heard the sound of the pebbles glorifying.(11) The Noble Messenger صلى الله عليه وآله said: How many mounts are better than their riders, because they remember God more often.(12)

Regarding the glorification of the creatures of existence, several points must be noted: A) The Quran considers the glorification of creatures to be conscious and based on knowledge and awareness: «كلٌّ قد علم صلاته و تسبیحه»(13) B) Not everyone can comprehend the glorification of other creatures: «ولكن لا تفقهون تسبیحهم»(14) C) The glorification of creatures is varied. Imam Sajjad عليه السلام narrated from Amir al-Mu'minin عليه السلام that: Birds glorify Allah every morning and ask for their daily sustenance.(15)

Nizami has composed verses on this topic: Do you know why the travelers of the heavens Revolve around the center of the earth? What do they seek by cutting ties with this abode? What do they search for by pulling this litter? Who is their object of worship in this sanctuary? What is their purpose in this coming and going? All are wandering like a compass, Seeking their own Creator.

Hafez of Shiraz also says: There is no sight that is not illuminated by the light of Your countenance; There is no human being upon whom the favor of the dust of Your threshold does not rest. Those who possess insight are observers of Your face, indeed; The secret of Your tresses is not absent from any head.

Hajji Sabzevari, the author of the book Manzumah, also says: There is no Moses to hear the claim of Ana al-Haqq (I am the Truth), Otherwise, this murmur is not absent from any tree.

Rumi (Mawlawi) also says: All the particles of the world secretly say to you, day and night: We are hearing, seeing, and aware, But to you, the strangers, we are silent.

1) Tur, 43. 2) Qalam, 29. 3) An'am, 91. 4) Ali 'Imran, 191. 5) Mu'minun, 115. 6) Tafsir al-Mizan, Vol. 10, p. 30. 7) Fath, 1-3. 8) Saffat, 143. 9) Bihar, Vol. 93, p. 177. 10) Wafi, Vol. 2, p. 157. 11) Tafsir al-Mizan, Vol. 13, p. 96. 12) Tafsir al-Mizan. 13) Nur, 41. 14) Isra, 44. 15) Tafsir al-Mizan.

Messages

  1. In the divine worldview, existence is glorifying God. «یسبّح للّه ما فى السموات و ما فى الارض»
  2. In cosmic glorification, there is no difference between the creatures of the heavens and the earth, between inanimate objects, plants, animals, and humans. «یسبّح للّه ما فى السموات و ما فى الارض»
  3. Praise must have a standard, measure, and reason. Glorification is only worthy of the One Who possesses sovereignty, holiness, might, and wisdom simultaneously. «یسبّح للّه... الملك القدّوس العزیز الحكیم»
  4. The kingship and sovereignty of Allah are accompanied by holiness, power, and wisdom. «الملك القدّوس»
  5. The governance of Allah is accompanied by wisdom. «الملك... الحكیم»
  6. Allah is Mighty (Aziz). «العزیز الحكیم» (Just as His Prophet, those who believe in His Prophet, and His Quran are mighty. «و للّه العزّة و لرسوله و للمؤمنین»(16), «و انّه لكتاب عزیز»(17))
  7. Power is valuable when accompanied by wisdom. «العزیز الحكیم» Allah is both Powerful and Wise, but the power of others is usually accompanied by the spirit of arrogance (istikbar) and colonialism. 16) Munafiqun, 8. 17) Fussilat, 41.

Explanations

Portrait of Surah Al-Jumu'ah Surah Al-Jumu'ah has 11 verses and was revealed in Medina. One of the fundamental goals of this Surah is to encourage, motivate, and urge Muslims to participate unitedly in the weekly congregation of Jumu'ah (Friday) Prayer. This Surah begins with the glorification of Allah and the declaration of the mission of the Prophet of Islam. It then asks Muslims not to be like the Jews, who were given the Torah but failed to bear it and act upon it. It asks Muslims that whenever the call for Jumu'ah Prayer is raised, they should abandon all work and hasten towards the remembrance of God, otherwise they deserve reproach and blame. The recitation of Surah Al-Jumu'ah is recommended on Friday night and in the first rak'ah (unit) of the Jumu'ah Prayer.

Ayah

هُوَ ٱلَّذِى بَعَثَ فِى ٱلْأُمِّيِّـۧنَ رَسُولًا مِّنْهُمْ يَتْلُوا۟ عَلَيْهِمْ ءَايَـٰتِهِۦ وَيُزَكِّيهِمْ وَيُعَلِّمُهُمُ ٱلْكِتَـٰبَ وَٱلْحِكْمَةَ وَإِن كَانُوا۟ مِن قَبْلُ لَفِى ضَلَـٰلٍ مُّبِينٍ (۲)
It is He who sent to the unlettered [people] an apostle from among themselves, to recite to them His signs, to purify them, and to teach them the Book and wisdom, and earlier they had indeed been in manifest error. (2)

Key Points

The word ummi (اُمّى) is derived from the word umm (اُمّ, mother). This word is applied to the people of Mecca either because Mecca is Umm al-Qura (Mother of all Settlements), or because in the Arabic language, those who do not know how to read and write are called ummi, as they are like the day they were born from their mother. In a hadith from Imam Sadiq عليه السلام, we read: Because the people of Mecca did not have a heavenly book or a heavenly leader, Allah attributed the term ummi to them; otherwise, some of them were capable of writing. (18)

Yuzakkīhim (یزكّیهم) is from the verbal noun tazkiyah (تزكیه), and its root, zakāt (زكات), means growth and development accompanied by goodness and blessing. This means the Prophet is commanded to remove evil morals from among the people and promote good morals among them so that they may reach growth and perfection.

The meaning of al-Hikmah (حكمت) here is the knowledge and realities derived from the verses of the Quran, which prepare the ground for human happiness by changing man's outlook on the world and giving him correct insight. For instance, deniers of God used to say: «ما هى الاّ حیاتنا الدنیا» (19) (Our life is limited to this world). But Allah introduces the reality as something else and says: «ما هذه الحیاة الدنیا الاّ لهو و لعب و ان الدار الآخرة لهى الحیوان» (20), (This world is nothing but diversion and amusement, and the Home of the Hereafter is the true life). (21)

Al-Hikmah is not separate from al-Kitāb (the Book); rather, it is one of its attributes, just as He says elsewhere: «و القرآن الحكیم» (22) (By the Wise Quran). And Allah has also revealed the Book and the Wisdom together to the Prophet: «و انزل اللّه علیك الكتاب و الحكمة» (23). Therefore, the purpose of teaching the Book and the Wisdom, which is the duty of the Prophet, «یعلّمهم الكتاب و الحكمة», is the teaching of the Quran, which is full of wisdom and instructs people in the correct insight for living. (24)

In Ayah 129 of Surah Al-Baqarah, Prophet Ibrahim عليه السلام asked Allah to raise a Prophet among the people with the characteristics mentioned in this Ayah; in this Ayah, we see the fulfillment of that prayer. In a hadith, we read that the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله said: «أنا دعوة أبى ابراهیم» meaning, "I am the answered prayer of my father Ibrahim."

In the previous Ayah, the discussion was about Allah, the Wise (Al-Hakīm). In this Ayah, one of the signs of God's wisdom is introduced as the sending of the Prophet, and one of the reasons for sending the Prophets is the teaching of wisdom to the people.

In the Quran, misguidance (ḍalālah), deviation, and error are mentioned forty-six times. In most cases, the word ḍalāl (ضلال) is accompanied by mubīn (manifest), and sometimes it is expressed as ḍalāl kabīr (ضلال كبیر) (great misguidance) (25) and ḍalāl ba‘īd (ضلال بعید) (far misguidance) (26). With a brief look, we realize that the Noble Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله guided the people: from infanticide to loving children, from ignorance to knowledge, from plundering the wealth of others to self-sacrifice and giving away their own wealth, from usury to lawful business, from polytheism to monotheism, from dispersion to unity, from superstitions to truth, from humiliation to honor, and in a word, from misguidance to guidance.

As Ja'far ibn Abi Talib, in the presence of the Negus, the King of Abyssinia, said in describing the Prophet of Islam: We were a people who worshipped idols, ate carrion, committed shameful deeds, severed ties of kinship, disregarded the rights of neighbors, and the strong showed no mercy to the weak... until Allah raised a Prophet from among us, and He commanded us towards human values. «و ان كانوا من قبل لفى ضلال مبین»

Question: Was the Prophet of Islam صلى الله عليه وآله sent only to the ummiyyīn, the people of Hijaz, and the Arabs? Answer: Prophet Muhammad صلى الله عليه وآله appeared among the people of Mecca and began His call there, but the letters He sent to the kings of Persia and Rome and the leaders of various tribes indicate the reality that His call was not limited to the Arabian Peninsula or the Arab nation.

18) Tafsir Nur al-Thaqalayn. 19) Al-Jathiyah, 24. 20) Al-Ankabut, 64. 21) Tafsir Al-Mizan. 22) Ya Sin, 2. 23) An-Nisa, 113. 24) Tafsir Al-Mizan. 25) Al-Mulk, Ayah 9. 26) Ibrahim, 3.

Messages

1- The sending of Prophets is a divine act. «هو الّذى بعث»

2- The sending of Prophets is a factor for growth, leap, and resurrection at all levels and affairs of society. «بعث»

3- The rise of the Prophet from an ummi society, and subsequently His championing of knowledge and wisdom, is a divine miracle. «بعث فى الامّیین رسولاً»

4- The leader of the Islamic society must be from the people, «رسولاً منهم» (a Messenger from among them), and among the people. «فى الامّیین»

5- The recitation of the Quran is the means and prerequisite for purification (tazkiyah), and purification precedes teaching and learning. «یتلوا... یزكّیهم... یعلّمهم». (Just as we read elsewhere: «اتّقوا اللّه و یعلّمكم اللّه» (27) Fear Allah so that Allah may teach you realities.)

6- Purification and self-building must be under the guidance of the school of the Prophets and divine verses. (Ascetic practices and monasticism that are not under the guidance of the Prophets' teachings are themselves a form of deviation.) «یتلوا علیهم آیاته و یزكّیهم»

7- Purification and teaching are at the forefront of the Prophets' programs. «یزكّیهم و یعلّمهم»

8- In the school of the Prophets, spiritual nurturing and intellectual training are presented alongside insight and discernment. «یزكّیهم و یعلّمهم الكتاب و الحكمة»

9- Educational work can be done even in a corrupt environment. «و ان كانوا من قبل لفى ضلال مبین»

10- To appreciate the efforts of the divine Prophets, attention must be paid to the cultural and moral condition of the people of their time. «و ان كانوا من قبل لفى ضلال مبین»

27) Al-Baqarah, 282.

Ayah

وَءَاخَرِينَ مِنْهُمْ لَمَّا يَلْحَقُوا۟ بِهِمْ ۚ وَهُوَ ٱلْعَزِيزُ ٱلْحَكِيمُ (۳)
And to others from among them [as well] who have not yet joined them. And He is the All-mighty, the All-wise. (3)

Ayah

ذَٰلِكَ فَضْلُ ٱللَّهِ يُؤْتِيهِ مَن يَشَآءُ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ ذُو ٱلْفَضْلِ ٱلْعَظِيمِ (٤)
That is Allah’s grace, which He grants to whomever He wishes, and Allah is dispenser of a great grace. (4)

Key Points

We read in Shia and Sunni commentaries: When this Ayah was revealed, the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله, in response to the question of who is meant by "the others" (آخرین) in the third Ayah, placed His blessed hand upon Salman's shoulder and said: "Even if faith were suspended in the Pleiades star cluster (Thurayya), men from the lineage and tribe of this man would go after it."

Messages

1- The mission of the Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله is not exclusive to the people of his time, but includes all people after him, regardless of race or region. «و آخرین منهم لمّا یلحقوا...»

2- No one will have the power to prevent the spread of Islam in the future of history. «و آخرین منهم لمّا یلحقوا... و هو العزیز»

3- The support for the prophets is the power and wisdom of Allah. «بعث فى الامّیین... و هو العزیز الحكیم»

4- The same wisdom that was the reason for sending prophets to previous generations is also present in the mission of the Prophet of Islam. «العزیز الحكیم... بعث فى الامیین... و آخرین منهم... و هو العزیز الحكیم»

5- Although prophethood is a great favor and status that Allah grants to whomever He wills, because He is Wise (Hakim), He grants this status only to those who are worthy of it. «العزیز الحكیم ذلك فضل اللّه یؤتیه من یشاء»

Ayah

مَثَلُ ٱلَّذِينَ حُمِّلُوا۟ ٱلتَّوْرَىٰةَ ثُمَّ لَمْ يَحْمِلُوهَا كَمَثَلِ ٱلْحِمَارِ يَحْمِلُ أَسْفَارًۢا ۚ بِئْسَ مَثَلُ ٱلْقَوْمِ ٱلَّذِينَ كَذَّبُوا۟ بِـَٔايَـٰتِ ٱللَّهِ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ لَا يَهْدِى ٱلْقَوْمَ ٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (٥)
The example of those who were charged with the Torah, then failed to carry it, is that of an ass carrying books. Evil is the example of the people who deny Allah’s signs, and Allah does not guide the wrongdoing lot. (5)

Key Points

The previous verses discussed the Prophetic mission (Bi'that), and this verse speaks about the Jews who, based on their heavenly book, the Torah, recognized the Holy Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله but showed obstinacy and refused to accept His prophethood. Asfār (أسفار) is the plural of Sifr (سِفر), meaning a book that unveils realities. (28) The Torah is composed of various sections, and each section, which is similar to a Surah of the Quran, is called a Sifr, such as the Book of Genesis (Sifr-e Paydāyish).

Although the Quran criticizes the Jews in this verse, it is actually an allusion to the Muslims, warning them not to listen to the Quran but fail to act upon it. The Quran has likened unworthy people to livestock in several instances, one of which is the scholars who do not practice their knowledge, as referenced in this verse: «اولئك كالانعام بل هم اضل» (29)

Sa'di says: However much knowledge you read, If action is not within you, you are ignorant. He is neither a researcher nor a scholar, But a beast of burden carrying several books. What knowledge or news does that empty-headed one possess, Whether he carries firewood or volumes?

Imam Ali عليه السلام said: The scholar who acts upon his knowledge is among the saved, and the one who does not act upon his knowledge will be among the doomed and regretful. The inhabitants of Hell will be tormented by his foul odor. The greatest regret and remorse in Hell will belong to the person who invited people toward Allah, and they accepted his word, obeyed Allah, and entered Paradise, but the inviter himself goes to Hell because he was afflicted by carnal desires and long aspirations. «انّ اشدّ اهل النّار ندامةً و حسرةً رجل دعا عبدا الى اللّه فاستجاب و قبل منه و اطاع اللّه فادخله اللّه الجنّة و ادخل الدّاعى النّار بتركه علمه و اتّباعه الهوى و طول الأمل» (30)

Imam Sadiq عليه السلام said: «انّ العالم اذا لم یعمل بعلمه زلّت موعظته عن القلوب كما یزلّ المطر عن الصفا» (31) The admonition of a scholar who does not act upon his knowledge slips away from hearts, just as rain slips away from a smooth rock.

Imam Sajjad عليه السلام said: It is stated in the Gospel: «انّ العلم اذا لم یعمل به لم یزدد صاحبه الاّ كفرا و لم یزدد من اللّه الاّ بُعداً» (32) Nothing is added to the scholar who does not act upon his knowledge except disbelief (kufr) and distance from Allah.

In this verse, a sharp and warning analogy is presented regarding religious scholars who do not practice their knowledge. The religious leaders have also expressed many analogies and comparisons concerning this matter, some of which we mention here:

  • The scholar without action is like a candle from whose light others benefit, but which destroys itself. (33)
  • The scholar without action is like a treasure that is not spent (in charity). (34)
  • The wicked scholar is like a rock placed in the path of water; he neither drinks from it himself nor allows the water to reach others. (35)
  • The scholar without action is like a sewage well whose interior is putrid. (36)
  • The scholar without action is like a grave whose exterior is sound but whose interior contains rotten bones. (37)

A Portrait of the Ugly Features of the Jews in the Quran

  • They are hard-hearted: «من الّذین هادوا... اولئك الّذین لم یرد اللّه ان یطهر قلوبهم» (38)
  • They are comfort-seeking: «لن نصبر على طعام واحد» (39)
  • They spread corruption on earth: «یسعون فى الارض فسادا» (40)
  • They even slander Allah: «ید اللّه مغلولة» (41)
  • They distort the Divine Book: «یحرّفون الكلم عن مواضعه» (42)
  • Whenever revelation does not suit their desires, they show arrogance: «افكلما جاءكم رسول بما لا تهوى انفسكم استكبرتم» (43)
  • They harbor the greatest enmity toward the people of faith: «اشدّ الناس عداوة للذین آمنوا الیهود» (44)
  • They used to kill the prophets: «لقد اخذنا میثاق بنى اسرائیل و ارسلنا الیهم رسلاً... فریقاً كذّبوا و فریقاً یقتلون» (45)
  • They incurred the wrath of Allah: «و باؤوا بغضب من اللّه» (46)
  • A group of them were cursed by Prophet Jesus and Prophet David: «لُعِن الّذین كفروا من بنى اسرائیل على لسان داود و عیسى» (47)

28) Mufradāt Rāghib. 29) Al-A‘rāf, 179. 30) Kāfī, Vol. 1, p. 44. 31) Kāfī, Vol. 1, p. 44. 32) Kāfī, Vol. 1, p. 45. 33) Mīzān al-Hikmah, Vol. 9, p. 54. 34) and 4. Mīzān al-Hikmah, Vol. 9, p. 55. 35) 36) and 6. Mahajjat al-Bayḍā’, Vol. 1, p. 130. 37) 38) Al-Mā’idah, 41. 39) Al-Baqarah, 61. 40) Al-Mā’idah, 64. 41) Al-Mā’idah, 64. 42) An-Nisā’, 46. 43) Al-Baqarah, 87. 44) Al-Mā’idah, 82. 45) Al-Mā’idah, 70. 46) Al-Baqarah, 61. 47) Al-Mā’idah, 78.

Messages

  1. Using analogy (tamthīl) to express realities is a Quranic method. (The Quran likens scholars without action to a donkey, which is outwardly heavy but inwardly light/worthless). «مثل الّذین حمّلوا التوراة... كمثل الحمار یحمل اسفاراً»

  2. Exposing corrupt scholars and speaking out in criticism against them is a Quranic imperative. «مثل الّذین حمّلوا التوراة ثم لم یحملوها كمثل الحمار یحمل اسفاراً»

  3. If failing to act upon the Torah degrades a person to the level of an animal like a donkey, what will failing to act upon the Quran, which is the superior Book, do to a person? «كمثل الحمار»

  4. Possessing and carrying the heavenly Book is not important; acting upon it is important. «لم یحملوها... یحمل اسفاراً»

  5. Failing to act upon one's knowledge is a form of practical denial (takdhīb) of it. «بئس مثل القوم الّذین كذّبوا»

  6. Failing to act upon knowledge is a form of oppression (ẓulm). «و اللّه لایهدى القوم الظالمین»

  7. The reason for deprivations is our own actions. «و اللّه لایهدى القوم الظالمین»

Ayah

قُلْ يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ هَادُوٓا۟ إِن زَعَمْتُمْ أَنَّكُمْ أَوْلِيَآءُ لِلَّهِ مِن دُونِ ٱلنَّاسِ فَتَمَنَّوُا۟ ٱلْمَوْتَ إِن كُنتُمْ صَـٰدِقِينَ (٦)
Say, ‘O Jews! If you claim that you are Allah’s favourites, to the exclusion of other people, then long for death, should you be truthful.’ (6)

Ayah

وَلَا يَتَمَنَّوْنَهُۥٓ أَبَدًۢا بِمَا قَدَّمَتْ أَيْدِيهِمْ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ عَلِيمٌۢ بِٱلظَّـٰلِمِينَ (۷)
Yet they will never long for it, because of what their hands have sent ahead, and Allah knows best the wrongdoers. (7)

Key Points

In some verses of the Quran, it is mentioned that the Jews considered themselves superior to others and even regarded themselves as the children of God, raising the slogan, «نحن ابناء اللّه» (We are the children of God) (48), despite being a people who hoarded wealth and loved the world, as the Quran states: «احرص النّاس على حیاة» (The most covetous of people for life) (49). This verse, as it were, performs Mubahalah (a challenge/imprecation) with them, stating that if you are truthful in claiming to be the friends (Awliya) of God, you must welcome death. Since you claim that only you are the people of Paradise, «لن یدخل الجنّة الاّ من كان هوداً او نصارى» (None shall enter Paradise except one who is a Jew or a Christian) (50), then why are you unwilling to wish for death? «قل ان كانت لكم الدار الآخرة عنداللّه خالصة من دون النّاس فتمنّوا الموت ان كنتم صادقین» (Say: If the abode of the Hereafter with Allah is exclusively for you to the exclusion of other people, then wish for death, if you are truthful) (51).

Why do we fear death? If we liken the journey towards the Hereafter to worldly travels, we see that a driver's anxieties during a trip can stem from various reasons:

A) Sometimes the anxiety is due to a lack of fuel, but one who has gathered provisions for his Hereafter is not worried. «تزوّدوا فانّ خیر الزّاد التقوى» (And take provision, for indeed, the best provision is righteousness) (52). B) Sometimes the anxiety is due to the vehicle being stolen (illegally acquired), but one whose income is lawful (halal) is not worried. C) Sometimes the anxiety is for the smuggled goods he carries, but one who does not bear the burden of sin is not worried. D) Sometimes the anxiety is for unauthorized speed, but one who is not given to excess or negligence, «كان امره فرطا» (whose affair is [a matter of] neglect) (53), and whose life has been based on moderation and temperance, «لم یسرفوا و لم یقتروا و كان بین ذلك قواماً» (who, when they spend, are neither extravagant nor miserly, but hold a just balance between those [extremes]) (54), has no fear. E) Sometimes the anxiety is due to unknown and wicked travel companions, but one who has been a companion and associate of the righteous (abrar) in this world is not worried. F) Sometimes he fears being alone on the road, but one who is in the caravan of the righteous and whose connection is with the believers is not alone. G) Sometimes he has fear and concern about not reaching the destination, but one who believes that not an atom's weight of his deed will be wasted is not worried. «فمن یعمل مثقال ذرّة...» (So whoever does an atom's weight of good...) (55).

They asked Abu Dharr: Why do we fear death? He replied: «انّكم عمّرتم الدنیا و خرّبتم الاخرة فتكرهون ان تنتقلوا من عمران الى خراب» (You have built up the world and ruined the Hereafter, so you dislike moving from prosperity to ruin) (56). You have built up the world and ruined the Hereafter, so you dislike moving from prosperity to ruin.

Imam Ali عليه السلام used to say: «واللّه ابن ابیطالب آنس بالموت من الطفل بثدى امّه» (By Allah, the son of Abu Talib is more familiar with death than an infant is with his mother's breast) (57). By God, my (Ali's) familiarity with death is greater than an infant's familiarity with his mother's breast. And when he was struck in the Mihrab (prayer niche) of the Kufa Mosque, he said: «فزت و ربّ الكعبة» (By the Lord of the Ka'bah, I have succeeded/triumphed).

Imam Hussain عليه السلام, in a detailed sermon, states: «خُطّ الموت على ولد آدم مخطّ القلادة على جید الفتاة» (Death has been decreed upon the children of Adam like a necklace upon the neck of a young woman) (58). Death is beautiful for the children of Adam, like a necklace on the necks of brides.

Although the address in this verse is directed at the Jews, readiness for death is a means of testing all claimants of faith and divine guardianship (Wilayah). «فتمنّوا الموت» (Then wish for death).

48) Ma'idah, 18. 49) Baqarah, 96. 50) Baqarah, 111. 51) Baqarah, 94. 52) Baqarah, 197. 53) Kahf, 28. 54) Furqan, 67. 55) Zilzal, 6 and 7. 56) Mahajjat al-Bayda', Vol. 8, p. 258. 57) Nahj al-Balaghah, Sermon 5. 58) Maqtal al-Khwarazmi, Vol. 2, p. 241.

Messages

1- Allah teaches the Prophet and His Ummah the method of discussion and argumentation with other religions and disputation with opponents. «قل یا ایّها الّذین هادوا» (Say, "O you who are Jews").

2- Respond to doubts and baseless assumptions with the logic of conscience. «ان زعمتم انّكم اولیاء للّه» (If you claim that you are the friends of Allah).

3- Exclusivity in spiritual and religious matters is forbidden. «من دون الناس» (to the exclusion of other people).

4- Excessive ambitions and misplaced fantasies must be curbed. «ان زعمتم... فتمنّوا الموت» (If you claim... then wish for death).

5- The criterion for sincerity in claiming faith is readiness for death. «فتمنّوا الموت ان كنتم صادقین» (Then wish for death, if you are truthful). Every friend is eager to meet his friend; if you are the friends of God, why are you not eager to meet Him? «ان زعمتم انّكم اولیاء للّه... فتمنّوا الموت» (If you claim that you are the friends of Allah... then wish for death).

6- Argue in such a way that the enemy has nothing left to say. «فتمنّوا الموت... و لا یتمنونه ابداً» (Then wish for death... but they will never wish for it).

7- The root of fleeing from death lies in the deeds of the people themselves. «و لایتمنّونه ابداً بما قدّمت ایدیهم» (But they will never wish for it, because of what their hands have put forth).

8- Perhaps some falsely claim to be the friends of Allah (Awliya Allah), but Allah is aware of their impure inner reality. «و اللّه علیم بالظالمین» (And Allah is Knowing of the wrongdoers).

Ayah

قُلْ إِنَّ ٱلْمَوْتَ ٱلَّذِى تَفِرُّونَ مِنْهُ فَإِنَّهُۥ مُلَـٰقِيكُمْ ۖ ثُمَّ تُرَدُّونَ إِلَىٰ عَـٰلِمِ ٱلْغَيْبِ وَٱلشَّهَـٰدَةِ فَيُنَبِّئُكُم بِمَا كُنتُمْ تَعْمَلُونَ (۸)
Say, ‘The death that you flee will indeed encounter you. Then you will be returned to the Knower of the sensible and the Unseen, and He will inform you about what you used to do.’ (8)

Key Points

Humans devise thousands of schemes to escape death, but to what avail? They flee from the battlefield, but Allah says: This flight will never prevent your being killed or dying. «قل لن ینفعكم الفرار»(59) They build very strong houses, yet death still comes to them. «اینما تكونوا یدرككم الموت و لو كنتم فى بروجٍ مشیّدة»(60) Yes, in this world one cannot escape death, just as in the Hereafter one cannot escape divine wrath and punishment. «یقول الانسان یومئذٍ این المفرّ»(61) Faith and belief that we will be resurrected and present on the Day of Judgment, and that crimes will be exposed, is the best factor for observing piety (taqwa) in this world. «فینبّئكم»

59) Al-Ahzab, 16. 60) An-Nisa, 78. 61) Al-Qiyamah, 10.

Messages

1- The Will of Allah prevails over the will of man. «تفرّون... ملاقیكم»

2- There is a long interval between death and the Day of Judgment. «فانّه ملاقیكم ثمّ تردّون» (The word «ثمّ» [thumma] is used to denote delay.)

3- Allah's knowledge regarding the present and the absent, the manifest and the hidden, is uniform. «عالم الغیب و الشهادة»

4- The criminal must first be informed of his crime, and then receive the punishment. «فینبّئكم بما كنتم تعملون»

Ayah

يَـٰٓأَيُّهَا ٱلَّذِينَ ءَامَنُوٓا۟ إِذَا نُودِىَ لِلصَّلَوٰةِ مِن يَوْمِ ٱلْجُمُعَةِ فَٱسْعَوْا۟ إِلَىٰ ذِكْرِ ٱللَّهِ وَذَرُوا۟ ٱلْبَيْعَ ۚ ذَٰلِكُمْ خَيْرٌ لَّكُمْ إِن كُنتُمْ تَعْلَمُونَ (۹)
O you who have faith! When the call is made for prayer on Friday, hurry toward the remembrance of Allah, and leave all business. That is better for you, should you know. (9)

Key Points

The noble Messenger of Islam (صلى الله عليه وآله) observed during the Mi'raj (Ascension) that angels seek forgiveness for those who attend the Friday Prayer and perform the Friday Ghusl (ritual bath), saying: "O Allah, forgive those who attend the Friday Prayer. O Allah, forgive those who perform Ghusl on Friday." (62)

The Status of Friday in Islam Among the days of the week, Friday holds special importance, and there are many narrations regarding it, some of which are mentioned below:

  • The Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله) said: Friday is the chief of the days of the week and holds the greatest rank in the sight of Allah the Almighty. (63)
  • Friday is the day for helping the deprived and the poor. We read in a Hadith that you should pay your charity and alms on Friday. (64)
  • Friday is the Eid (festival) of the Muslims. (65)
  • Friday is the day of the appearance of Imam Mahdi (عليه السلام) and the end of His occultation: "Our Qā'im (Riser) will emerge on Friday." (66)
  • On Friday, the gates of heaven are opened for the ascent of the servants' deeds. (67)
  • The Prophet says: On Friday, Allah multiplies good deeds and erases evil deeds, increases the ranks of the believers, answers prayers, wards off calamities, and fulfills the needs of the believers. (68)
  • Whoever visits the grave of his parents or one of them on Friday, his sins are forgiven, and his name is written among the righteous. (69)
  • Do not neglect the Friday Ghusl on Friday, for it is the Sunnah (tradition) of the Prophet of Islam (صلى الله عليه وآله), and use perfume and wear good clothes. (70)
  • The Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله) said: Shall I inform you who the people of Paradise are? He who is not deterred from the deeds of Friday by intense heat or cold. (71)
  • Perform good deeds on Friday as much as you can. "And let him do good as much as he is able." (72)
  • For the one who cleanses himself on Friday, clips his nails and hair (head and face), brushes his teeth... and proceeds to the Friday Prayer, seventy thousand angels accompany him and seek forgiveness and intercession for him. (73)

The Status of Friday Prayer The first action taken by the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله) after migrating to Medina was establishing the Friday Prayer.

  • A prayer whose invitation begins with the phrase, "O you who have believed."
  • A prayer whose performance people are encouraged to attend with the phrase, "Hasten to the remembrance of Allah."
  • A prayer that carries the command to abandon trade and transaction.
  • A prayer that is considered equal to Hajj in narrations. "You must attend the Friday Prayer, for it is the Hajj of the poor." (74)
  • A prayer after whose sermon supplication is answered. (75)
  • A prayer that is established while the Friday Imam leans on a weapon.
  • A prayer whose disregard and contempt are considered a sign of hypocrisy.
  • A prayer in which the Friday Imam must enjoin the people to piety.
  • A prayer in which people become aware of global issues.
  • A prayer that must be performed in congregation and cannot be performed individually.
  • A prayer for which it is recommended that necessary facilities be provided for the attendance of those imprisoned for financial debts. (76)
  • A prayer for which it is appropriate that travel be postponed in order to attend it. Imam Ali (عليه السلام) states in a letter to Harith al-Hamdani: Do not travel on Friday until you have attended the prayer, unless it is a journey in the way of Allah or a task for which you have a valid excuse. (77)
  • A prayer for which it is recommended that you prepare yourself from the day before. Imam Ali (عليه السلام) used to forbid people from taking medicine on Thursday that would cause weakness (and prevent participation in the prayer) on Friday. (78) And Imam Baqir (عليه السلام), describing the companions of the Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله), said: They used to prepare themselves for the Friday Prayer starting from Thursday. (79)
  • A prayer whose reward for the traveler attending it is greater than the reward for those who reside in that same city. (80)
  • A prayer for which the recitation of its verses is recommended the night before its performance. In narrations, it is recommended that Surah Al-Jumu'ah be recited in the Maghrib Prayer on the night preceding Friday, which serves as a reminder and preparation for the Friday Prayer.
  • The Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله) said: For every step a believer takes toward the Friday Prayer, Allah reduces the terror and fear of the Day of Resurrection for him. (81)
  • The Friday Prayer is an expiation for the sins a person commits throughout the week, provided he avoids major sins. (82)
  • Whoever hires a worker must not prevent him from going to the Friday Prayer, for he commits a sin. (83)
  • Whoever abandons the Friday Prayer for three consecutive Fridays without a valid excuse, his name is recorded as a hypocrite. "Whoever abandons the Friday Prayer three times consecutively without an excuse is written down as a hypocrite." (84) And in another narration, we read that whoever fails to attend the Friday Prayer for three Fridays, other than due to illness or another valid excuse, Allah seals his heart. (85)
  • Whoever is unable to participate in the Friday Prayer should give a sum in charity (so that it may be an expiation for his absence from the Friday Prayer). (86)
  • The noble Messenger of Islam (صلى الله عليه وآله) said: Whoever abandons the Friday Prayer during my lifetime or after my death, out of denial or contempt and disregard, Allah will not gather his affairs, will not place blessing in his matters, and his Hajj and charity will not be accepted, unless he repents. (87)
  • When the Friday Imam stands to deliver the sermon, it is obligatory for the people to remain silent (88) and not perform prayer (89) and sit with good manners. (90)

Duties of the Friday Imam 1. The prayer sermons should not be long. The Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله) said: After you, there will come people who prolong the sermons and shorten the prayer: "There will come after you people who prolong the sermons and shorten the prayer." But you should prolong the prayer and shorten the sermons: "Prolong the prayer and shorten the sermon." (91) 2. Allah ordained two sermons in the Friday Prayer so that one of them is dedicated to the praise and glorification of Allah and the sanctification of God, and the other to the needs of the people, their warning, instruction, and supplication. (92) 3. He should deliver the sermon while standing. "I never saw the Messenger of Allah deliver a sermon except while standing." (93) 4. The speaker should hold a weapon but not use it; rather, he should engage in admonition and guidance. (Meaning: We possess power, but at the same time, we are compassionate and kind.) Just as the Prophet used to lean on a bow or a staff while delivering the sermon. "He would lean on a bow or a staff." (94) 5. When the Friday Imam ascends the pulpit and faces the people, he should greet them. "It is from the Sunnah that when the Imam ascends the pulpit, he should greet the people when he faces them." (95) 6. Reciting Surah Al-Jumu'ah and Al-Munafiqun in the Friday Prayer. The Prophet (صلى الله عليه وآله) used to recite Surah Al-Jumu'ah and Surah Al-Munafiqun in the Friday Prayer; Surah Al-Jumu'ah to give glad tidings to the believers and warn them against the world, and Surah Al-Munafiqun to disappoint and rebuke the hypocrites. (96) 7. Making the people aware of current issues. Imam Reza (عليه السلام) said: The Friday Prayer is a general gathering so that the Friday Imam may encourage the people to worship and obedience, restrain them from sin and disobedience, and inform them of what is happening around them and what is beneficial or harmful to them. "And he informs them of what has come upon them from the horizons and of the terrors in which there is harm and benefit for them." (97) 8. The Friday Imam should inform the people of what Allah has commanded and what He has forbidden, and what leads to their welfare and corruption, and he should teach them. "That he teaches them of His command and prohibition that contains welfare and corruption." (98)

62) مستدرك الوسائل، ج ۶، ص ۹۱. 63) وسائل الشیعه، ج ۵، ص ۱۷. 64) وسائل الشیعه، ج ۵، ص ۶۷. 65) وسائل الشیعه، ج ۵، ص ۶۶. 66) كمال الدین، ص ۱۶۴. 67) وسائل الشیعه، ج ۵، ص ۳۲۵. 68) وسائل الشیعه، ج ۵، ص ۶۳. 69) صلاة الجمعه، ص ۲۴۵. 70) كافى، ج ۳، ص ۴۱۷. 71) كنزالعمال، حدیث ۲۱۰۸۵. 72) وسائل الشیعه، ج ۵، ص ۷۸. 73) مستدرك، ج ۶، ص ۴۶. 74) تهذیب، ج ۳، ص ۲۳۷. 75) وسائل الشیعه، ج ۵، ص ۴۶. 76) وسائل الشیعه، ج ۵، ص ۳۶. 77) نهج البلاغه، نامه ۶۹. 78) بحارالانوار، ج ۸۹، ص ۱۹۷. 79) وسائل الشیعه، ج ۵، ص ۴۶. 80) وسائل الشیعه، ج ۵، ص ۳۶. 81) مستدرك الوسائل، ج ۶، ص ۴۲. 82) بحار الانوار، ج ۸۹، ص ۲۵۶. 83) مستدرك الوسائل، ج ۶، ص ۷. 84) مستدرك الوسائل، ج ۸، ص ۴۰۷. 85) كنز العمّال، حدیث ۲۱۱۴۷. 86) كنز العمّال، حدیث ۲۱۱۳۹. 87) مستدرك الوسائل، ج ۶، ص ۱۰. 88) بحارالانوار، ج ۸۹، ص ۲۵۶. 89) وسائل الشیعه، ج ۵، ص ۳۹. 90) كنز العمّال، ج ۷، ص ۷۴۶. 91) كنز العمّال، ج ۷، ص ۷۴۸. 92) وسائل الشیعه، ج ۵، ص ۴۰. 93) بحارالانوار، ج ۸۹، ص ۱۳۱. 94) وسائل الشیعه، ج ۵، ص ۳۸. 95) وسائل الشیعه، ج ۵، ص ۴۳. 96) كنز العمّال، ج ۸، ص ۳۷۸. 97) بحارالانوار، ج ۸۹، ص ۲۰۱. 98) وسائل الشیعه، ج ۵، ص ۴۰.

Messages

  1. To invite people to worship, preparation must be made. (At the beginning of the Surah, before the command to participate in the Friday Prayer, there is mention of the glorification of creation.) "Whatever is in the heavens glorifies Allah... hasten to the remembrance of Allah."

  2. To invite people, we should address them with respect and a good name. "O you who have believed."

  3. Attendance at the Friday Prayer is a sign of faith. "O you who have believed, when the call is made for the prayer..."

  4. Prayer is a means of remembering Allah. "When the call is made for the prayer... hasten to the remembrance of Allah."

  5. Hasten to attend the Friday Prayer. "for the prayer on the day of Friday, hasten."

  6. To attend the Friday Prayer, existing obstacles must be removed. "And leave trade." (Abandoning business and trade is mentioned as an example; otherwise, all activities must be abandoned at the time of prayer to attend the Friday Prayer.)

  7. Allah, who commands the abandonment of trade, compensates for it with true promises. "That is better for you, if you only knew."

  8. The sign of true knowledge is choosing the best options at all times. "That is better for you, if you only knew."

  9. In religious education, encouragement is necessary, "O you who have believed," and command is necessary, "Hasten to the remembrance of Allah," and prohibition is necessary, "And leave trade," and reasoning is necessary. "That is better for you."

Ayah

فَإِذَا قُضِيَتِ ٱلصَّلَوٰةُ فَٱنتَشِرُوا۟ فِى ٱلْأَرْضِ وَٱبْتَغُوا۟ مِن فَضْلِ ٱللَّهِ وَٱذْكُرُوا۟ ٱللَّهَ كَثِيرًا لَّعَلَّكُمْ تُفْلِحُونَ (۱٠)
And when the prayer is finished disperse through the land and seek Allah’s grace, and remember Allah much so that you may be felicitous. (10)

Key Points

In the culture of the Quran, worldly wealth is Divine favor (فضل الهى). Therefore, in this Ayah, He says: «و ابتغوا من فضل اللّه» (and seek the bounty of Allah), meaning that after the completion of the Friday Prayer, you should go seeking the favor of God, which means income and trade. Just as in another place, He considers it "good" (خير) and says: «ان ترك خیر الوصیّة» (if he leaves behind good, the testament) (99), meaning that if someone leaves behind some good, he should make a will, where "good" here refers to worldly wealth. The Noble Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله said: Whoever remembers Allah sincerely in the marketplace and place of business, while people are heedless of God and busy with their affairs, Allah records a thousand good deeds for him and grants him His special forgiveness on the Day of Resurrection. (100) Man's enthusiasm for worship must be greater than his enthusiasm for earning income. Regarding worship, He said: «فاسعوا الى ذكر اللّه» (then hasten to the remembrance of Allah), and in this Ayah, regarding trade, He says: «و ابتغوا من فضله» (and seek His bounty). Both words, «فاسعوا» (hasten) and «ابتغوا» (seek), mean effort and movement, but the first Ayah contains the word sa'y (سعى) and the letter fa (فاء), which implies love, enthusiasm, and speed. Divine favor (فضل الهى) is sometimes material and sometimes spiritual. In the fourth Ayah of this very Surah, regarding the mission of the Prophet صلى الله علیه وآله, He said: «ذلك فضل اللّه یؤتیه من یشاء» (That is the favor of Allah, He grants it to whom He wills), and in this Ayah, regarding earning and trade, He says: «و ابتغوا من فضل اللّه» (and seek the bounty of Allah).

99) Baqarah, 180. 100) Tafsir Majma' al-Bayan.

Messages

  1. The time for suspending business and trade for the sake of worship should not be unduly prolonged. (In the previous Ayah, the command to abandon trade was issued, and in this Ayah, immediately after the completion of worship, that prohibition is lifted). «فاذا قضیت الصّلاة فانتشروا فى الارض...»
  2. Allah does not approve of the Muslim market being closed and work being abandoned, even for a full day. «فاذا قضیت الصّلاة فانتشروا»
  3. Earning income and receiving Divine favor requires migration, movement, and travel throughout the land. «فانتشروا فى الارض»
  4. Be quick in performing worship, «فاسعوا» (hasten), but in material work, merely go seeking it, and there is no need for haste, because sustenance is decreed by Allah. «و ابتغوا» (and seek)
  5. The grace and favor of Allah are abundant, but man must strive to attain them. «و ابتغوا من فضل اللّه»
  6. Idleness is forbidden in Islam. «و ابتغوا من فضل اللّه»
  7. The world is a portion of Divine favor. «من فضل اللّه»
  8. Salvation (felicity) is achieved through carrying out Divine commands. «فاسعوا... ذروا... وابتغوا... و اذكروا... لعلّكم تفلحون»
  9. Striving to achieve both spiritual and material matters are commands from Allah. In the previous Ayah, He said: «فاسعوا الى ذكر اللّه» (then hasten to the remembrance of Allah), and in this Ayah, He says: «و ابتغوا من فضل اللّه» (and seek the bounty of Allah).
  10. If trade is based solely on human will, it is nothing more than a transaction, «و ذروا البیع» (and leave the sale), but if we go seeking trade after performing worship, it transforms into Divine favor. «و ابتغوا من فضل اللّه»
  11. Striving for the world should not be accompanied by heedlessness of the remembrance of Allah. «و ابتغوا من فضل اللّه واذكروا اللّه كثیراً»
  12. Islam desires both quality in worship, «فى صلاتهم خاشعون» (those who are humble in their prayers) (101), and quantity. «واذكروا اللّه كثیراً» (and remember Allah often)
  13. Where the potential for heedlessness and error is greater, such as the marketplace, the remembrance of Allah is more essential. «و ابتغوا من فضل اللّه واذكروا اللّه كثیراً»
  14. The remembrance of Allah is necessary at all times, whether during worship, «فاسعوا الى ذكر اللّه» (then hasten to the remembrance of Allah), or during trade. «و ابتغوا من فضل اللّه واذكروا اللّه»
  15. Man, through his good actions and choices, can prepare the ground for salvation. «فاسعوا... و ابتغوا...و اذكروا... لعلّكم تفلحون»
  16. The remembrance of Allah becomes a factor for salvation when it is continuous and constant. «واذكروا اللّه كثیراً لعلكم تفلحون» 101) Mu'minun, 2.

Ayah

وَإِذَا رَأَوْا۟ تِجَـٰرَةً أَوْ لَهْوًا ٱنفَضُّوٓا۟ إِلَيْهَا وَتَرَكُوكَ قَآئِمًا ۚ قُلْ مَا عِندَ ٱللَّهِ خَيْرٌ مِّنَ ٱللَّهْوِ وَمِنَ ٱلتِّجَـٰرَةِ ۚ وَٱللَّهُ خَيْرُ ٱلرَّٰزِقِينَ (۱۱)
When they sight a deal or a diversion, they scatter off towards it and leave you standing! Say, ‘What is with Allah is better than diversion and dealing, and Allah is the best of providers.’ (11)

Key Points

We read in the commentaries (Tafasir): The noble Prophet of Islam صلى الله علیه وآله was delivering the sermons (Khutbahs) of the Friday prayer when a trade caravan, accompanied by drums, tambourines, and noise, entered Medina. Most of the worshippers went toward the caravan and left the Prophet, who was standing and delivering the sermon, alone. This Ayah was revealed and reprimanded them. The Ayah, instead of criticizing the abandonment of the sermon and the prayer, criticizes the abandonment of the Prophet. Indeed, violating the sanctity of the divine leader is more serious than abandoning the sermon and the prayer. The beginning of this Surah was with the Prophetic mission (Bi'that) of the Prophet of Islam, and its end is with a criticism of leaving Him alone, and this itself is a warning to the believers.

Messages

1- The Quran criticizes greed and lack of piety (Taqwa) in business and earning. «و اذا رأوا... انفضّوا الیها»

2- The cause of deviation from the remembrance of Allah is either materialism or carnal desires (self-indulgence). «تجارة او لهواً»

3- The Friday speaker (Khateeb) must deliver the sermon while standing. «و تركوك قائماً»

4- Any action that causes the divine leader to be left alone is subject to reprimand. «و تركوك قائماً»

5- People's inclinations are not always correct or rightful. «و تركوك قائماً»

6- The two sermons are part of the Friday prayer, and listening to them is obligatory. «و تركوك قائماً»

7- In propagation (Tabligh), do not negate material matters, but rather emphasize the superiority of spiritual matters. «ما عنداللّه خیر من اللهو و من التجارة»

8- The criterion for values is a good ultimate outcome (Husn al-'Aqibah), not fleeting enjoyments. «ما عند اللّه خیر من اللهو و من التجارة»

9- Do not abandon worship to pursue trade for the sake of earning provision (Rizq). Rather, worship first and then pursue trade, for provision is in the hands of Allah. «ما عند اللّه خیر... و اللّه خیر الرازقین»

10- Trade is not the only way to obtain provision; if Allah wills, He will provide for you through other means as well. «و اللّه خیر الرازقین»

11- Allah has guaranteed the provision of those who attend the Friday prayer. «و اللّه خیر الرازقین»
